items.title: French Doors: Design Guide
items.description: Indoor vs outdoor, single vs double leaf, glazing and hardware options.

items.q: What types of doors does DECA manufacture?
items.a: We manufacture three main categories: sliding doors (PSk parallel-slide, Lift & Slide, and DECA Roto tilt & slide), swing/French doors (single and double-leaf with tilt & turn), and entry doors (insulated panel doors with multi-point locking).

All DECA doors feature multi-point locking systems (up to 12 points) and are available in RC2 and RC3 security classes. Entry doors include steel-reinforced frames and anti-drill, anti-pick cylinder locks.
Yes. Every door is made to order. Sliding doors can span up to 6 meters wide and 3 meters tall. Entry doors and French doors are available in virtually any dimension within structural limits.

items.q: What is the thermal performance of DECA doors?
items.a: Our door systems achieve U-values as low as 0.10 W/m²K with triple glazing. This exceeds Passive House requirements and delivers significant energy savings compared to standard American doors.
